hardware_context_rules.access_level_required: Internal Board Access (Attaching hardware programmer/clip to pins)
hardware_context_rules.target_vulnerability_pattern: Plaintext credentials, secret keys, or unencrypted configuration files
hardware_context_rules.simulated_exposure_vector: Extraction of plaintext system credentials, application configuration tokens, or unencrypted private keys from raw memory dumps.

hardware_context_rules.access_level_required: Local Casing Access (Plugging directly into exposed serial pins)
hardware_context_rules.target_vulnerability_pattern: Active bootloader shell, interactive kernel console, or unauthenticated local terminal daemons
hardware_context_rules.simulated_exposure_vector: Unauthenticated root console access spawned via initialization routines or vulnerable background terminal daemons during execution.

hardware_context_rules.access_level_required: Internal Board Access (Soldering/connecting to debug infrastructure)
hardware_context_rules.target_vulnerability_pattern: Direct memory access vulnerabilities, RAM dumping exploits, or unprotected processor register control
hardware_context_rules.simulated_exposure_vector: Full system exploitation via runtime memory manipulation, extraction of active cryptographic material from volatile RAM, or raw processor hijacking.

hardware_context_rules.access_level_required: Physical Device Interaction (Plugging external media or malicious peripherals)
hardware_context_rules.target_vulnerability_pattern: Device Firmware Update (DFU) binary modifications or unprotected external dynamic boot scripts
hardware_context_rules.simulated_exposure_vector: Execution of unauthorized or modified malicious third-party firmware binaries via side-loading or unauthenticated hardware update mechanisms.

hardware_context_rules.access_level_required: Local or Remote Access (Exploiting unpatched software hooks during runtime)
hardware_context_rules.target_vulnerability_pattern: Package management manifests, outdated system libraries, or unpatched core binary dependencies
hardware_context_rules.simulated_exposure_vector: Detection of outdated firmware components vulnerable to known public CVEs (e.g., Remote Code Execution). A version mismatch allows attackers to bypass software isolation and execute unauthorized commands directly on the hardware platform, compromising device integrity.
